relations.subject: White-headed robin-chat
relations.evidence: Rand|Rand]], 1955 | synonyms = }} The white-headed robin-chat (Cossypha heinrichi) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in northern Angola and the western Democratic Republic of the Congo

relations.subject: White-headed robin-chat
relations.evidence: In northern Angola, the white-headed robin-chat has only been recorded in two areas, including the type locality about north of Calandula. In the western Democratic Republic of the Congo, it has been recorded in Bombo-Lumene Forest Reserve and a few nearby sites. In Angola, the bird occurs in the undergrowth of gallery forests at an elevation of , and it is sometimes found in savannas

relations.subject: White-headed robin-chat
relations.evidence: It is threatened by habitat loss, and its IUCN conservation status is the least concern
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: White-headed robin-chat
relations.evidence: In Bombo-Lumene Forest Reserve, the species is threatened by deforestation for charcoal
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。
